statements.status_notes: Reviewed quay.io/stackstate/stackstate-k8s-agent:bedd24d7 on 2026-05-13. The embedded CPython 3.13.13 binary is present, but SUSE Observability agent source and packaged checks do not execute the vulnerable pattern: reusing lzma.LZMADecompressor, bz2.BZ2Decompressor, or gzip.GzipFile after MemoryError during decompression.
statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-6100 requires decompressor instance reuse after a MemoryError. Source review of stackstate-agent stackstate-7.71.2 and stackstate-agent-integrations found no affected Python decompressor API usage. Image inspection found only generic third-party one-shot or per-response decompression helpers; no packaged code catches MemoryError and resumes the same decompressor instance. A MemoryError propagates out of the check/request path and the affected decompressor object is discarded rather than reused.

statements.status_notes: Reviewed quay.io/stackstate/stackstate-k8s-agent:cb4d9ce2 on 2026-06-05. The image embeds CPython 3.13.13 and the vulnerable unicodedata.normalize implementation is present, but no unauthenticated or remote adversary-controlled input path was found that can drive it with crafted Unicode.
statements.impact_statement: The image embeds CPython 3.13.13 via the omnibus build (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3) and the vulnerable stdlib implementation is present in /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/. Source review of stackstate-agent cb4d9ce2e7 and stackstate-agent-integrations 7.71.2-3 found exactly one shipped first-party use of unicodedata.normalize: stackstate_checks_base/stackstate_checks/base/checks/base.py:559 inside AgentCheck.normalize(). The only production caller is _get_state_descriptor() at base.py:367-375, which normalizes the old-style check state descriptor string "instance.<type>.<url>". Metric submission paths do not call AgentCheck.normalize(): gauge/rate/count/monotonic_count/histogram submit metric names through _submit_metric()/aggregator, and tag normalization does not use unicodedata.normalize. The integration instance identity values reaching _get_state_descriptor() are local configuration or environment identity values: configured URLs for Dynatrace, ServiceNow, Splunk, and Zabbix; configured vSphere host; configured static topology/health file paths; or cluster/environment identity for kubelet and OpenMetrics. Third-party API responses and monitored service payloads were not found to flow into this sink. A privileged operator or cluster administrator with ability to alter agent configuration or cluster identity labels could provide a pathological string, and there is no generic length cap immediately before the normalize call, but that control is outside the remote or unauthenticated adversary model for this agent image. The supported runtime exposes DogStatsD and trace-agent ports, neither of which can influence the integration instance state descriptor.
statements.action_statement: Track a defense-in-depth hardening follow-up to bound integration instance state descriptor input before AgentCheck.normalize(), while keeping this finding VEXed because the vulnerable input is not adversary-controlled in supported deployments.

statements.impact_statement: The image embeds CPython 3.13.13, built from the upstream python.org tarball pinned at omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3 (default_version "3.13.13") and downloaded from https://python.org/ftp/python/3.13.13/Python-3.13.13.tgz at omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:16. Per the CVE intel, CVE-2025-12781 is fixed in CPython >=3.13.10,<3.14 (and >=3.14.1), so the shipped 3.13.13 is three patch releases past the fix line — the patched Lib/base64.py:b64decode/standard_b64decode/urlsafe_b64decode (which now reject the standard '+' / '/' characters when an alternate alphabet is supplied via altchars) is the copy installed at /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/lib/python3.13/base64.py inside the container. The omnibus build applies no patch that would revert that fix: the patches directory omnibus/config/patches/python3/ contains only CVE-2025-6965-sqlite-3.50.4.patch and CVE-2025-8194-tarfile.patch, neither of which touches Lib/base64.py, and the build only deletes the embedded test tree (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:66). No other Python interpreter version is pinned anywhere in omnibus/ or under Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile (verified by grep across omnibus/config/software/ and Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile). Independently, no first-party caller in the image even exercises the vulnerable altchars-aware code path: a recursive grep across ~/projects/stackstate-agent for the literal string 'altchars' returns zero matches in any .py file, and the only references to b64decode/standard_b64decode/urlsafe_b64decode in the entire stackstate-agent tree are tasks/libs/ciproviders/github_api.py:543 and tasks/libs/ciproviders/github_api.py:560 — both invoked only by the invoke-based CI tooling under tasks/, neither shipped into the runtime image (the Dockerfile extracts only opt/stackstate-agent and a few system binaries from the .deb at Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:11). The sibling stackstate-agent-integrations repo also has no altchars usage; the only b64decode call sites (stackstate_checks_dev/stackstate_checks/dev/_env.py:131, stackstate_checks_dev/stackstate_checks/dev/tooling/e2e/format.py:40, stackstate_checks_dev/tests/plugin/test_environment_runner.py:18) live under stackstate_checks_dev (a developer-only test harness) and call urlsafe_b64decode without an altchars argument, so they neither ship nor exercise the vulnerable branch. Defense in depth: the agent runs as USER 1000:1000 (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:71) with only DogStatsD/trace ports exposed (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:75), but is not material here because the vulnerable code is already fixed in the shipped interpreter.

The image bundles an upstream CPython 3.13.13 interpreter built from python.org sources via omnibus (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3, omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:14). Per the CVE intel, the 3.13.x branch is not yet listed among the fixed versions, so the vulnerable Lib/imaplib.py:IMAP4._command sink ships intact at /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/lib/python3.13/imaplib.py — the omnibus build only deletes the test/ subtree (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:66) and applies two unrelated CVE patches for sqlite and tarfile (omnibus/config/patches/python3/CVE-2025-6965-sqlite-3.50.4.patch, omnibus/config/patches/python3/CVE-2025-8194-tarfile.patch); no imaplib patch is applied. However, the imaplib module is never imported or invoked by any code that runs in the image. A repository-wide search for the canonical import statements ('import imaplib', 'from imaplib') against ~/projects/stackstate-agent returns zero matches, and a broader grep for 'imaplib' across the entire tree returns zero matches. The only 'imap'/'IMAP' substring hits are unrelated: Go 'multimap' identifiers in comp/core/autodiscovery/autodiscoveryimpl/multimap.go and comp/core/autodiscovery/autodiscoveryimpl/configmgr.go, eBPF kernel symbol tables in pkg/ebpf/c/vmlinux_*.h and pkg/ebpf/testdata/kallsyms.*, Windows perfmon counter names containing 'popimap' under pkg/collector/corechecks/system/testfiles/allcounters_en-us.txt, and a JUnit XML test fixture inside tasks/unit_tests/testdata/secret.tar.gz. None of the container entrypoints invoke an IMAP path either: Dockerfiles/agent/entrypoint.sh, Dockerfiles/agent/probe.sh, Dockerfiles/agent/cont-init.d/, Dockerfiles/agent/entrypoint.d/, and the secrets helper Dockerfiles/agent/secrets-helper/readsecret.py contain no 'imap' references. No StackState core check, integration, or rtloader binding pulls imaplib via the embedded interpreter (grep on rtloader/, internal/, tools/, comp/, pkg/, cmd/ returns only the false positives above). The vulnerable IMAP4._command sink also requires CR/LF in caller-supplied IMAP command arguments, which presupposes an imaplib-using program — none ships. Defense in depth: the agent process runs as the non-root UID 1000:1000 (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:71), and the image exposes no IMAP-related listener on its k8s service surface (Dockerfiles/agent/stackstate-kubernetes.yaml only declares the kubelet listener).

The image embeds a full CPython 3.13.13 interpreter via the omnibus build defined in omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3 (default_version "3.13.13"), shipped with the unmodified standard library, so Lib/poplib.py — and the vulnerable POP3._putcmd sink — is present on disk inside /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/. However, no first-party Go, Python, or shell code in stackstate-agent or stackstate-agent-integrations imports or otherwise references the poplib module: a recursive grep across both trees for `poplib`, `import poplib`, `from poplib`, and `POP3` returns zero matches. There is no agent check, integration, helper script, or entrypoint that opens a POP3 session, so POP3._putcmd is never reached at runtime. The Python interpreter shipped in the image is invoked exclusively by the agent's check loader (rtloader) to execute integrations under conf.d/checks.d, none of which exercise poplib, and CVSS PR:H confirms an attacker would additionally need authenticated control over POP3 command arguments — a surface this image does not expose (no POP3 client is constructed and no POP3 ports are listened on; the only EXPOSE directives in Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:75 are 8125/udp DogStatsD and 8126/tcp trace-agent). The container also runs as USER 1000:1000 per Dockerfile:71, further reducing residual risk.

The image embeds CPython built from the upstream tarball pinned at omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3 (default_version "3.13.13"). CVE-2026-1502 affects CPython 3.13.x strictly less than 3.13.10 (fixed in 3.13.10 — commits 05ed7ce7ae9e17c23a04085b2539fe6d6d3cef69 / b1cf9016335cb637c5a425032e8274a224f4b2ed land in the 3.13.10 release). The shipped 3.13.13 is three patch releases past the fix, so the patched Lib/http/client.py (HTTPConnection.set_tunnel/_tunnel CR/LF rejection) is the version installed at /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/lib/python3.13/http/client.py inside the container. The omnibus build does not apply any patch that would revert the fix — only omnibus/config/patches/python3/CVE-2025-6965-sqlite-3.50.4.patch and omnibus/config/patches/python3/CVE-2025-8194-tarfile.patch are listed (omnibus/config/patches/python3/), neither touches Lib/http/client.py, and the build only deletes the test/ subtree (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:66). No other Python interpreter version is pinned anywhere under omnibus/ or Dockerfiles/ (verified by grep across omnibus/ and Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile). Independently, no agent code calls the vulnerable sink: a tree-wide grep for set_tunnel returns only eBPF kernel-helper symbols (pkg/ebpf/bytecode/runtime/all_helpers.go:33,42; pkg/ebpf/c/bpf_helper_defs.h:497,534,572,837,848; pkg/ebpf/c/vmlinux_5_15_0.h:40701,40710,118881,118883; pkg/ebpf/c/vmlinux_5_15_0_arm.h:30213,30222) — none of which are CPython HTTPConnection.set_tunnel. So the image is fixed both because the embedded interpreter is past the fix release and because no shipped caller exercises the sink.

The image embeds CPython 3.13.13 (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3), which is within the vulnerable range for CVE-2026-4786 (>=3.13,<3.13.x-fixed). The vulnerable file Lib/webbrowser.py ships intact inside the embedded interpreter at /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/lib/python3.13/webbrowser.py — the omnibus build only strips the test/ subtree (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:66) and applies unrelated patches (omnibus/config/patches/python3/CVE-2025-6965-sqlite-3.50.4.patch, CVE-2025-8194-tarfile.patch). However, no code path that ships in the container imports or invokes webbrowser.UnixBrowser.open. A repo-wide grep for 'webbrowser' returns exactly one hit, .dda/extend/commands/run/docs/serve/__init__.py:34-36, which is a developer-only mkdocs serve helper inside the .dda/ dev-tool data directory; this directory is not referenced by Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile, Dockerfiles/cluster-agent/Dockerfile, or omnibus/config/projects/agent.rb and is not packaged into the stackstate-agent .deb that the image extracts (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:8-25). No agent core check, integration, entrypoint script (Dockerfiles/agent/entrypoint.sh, Dockerfiles/agent/cont-init.d/, /probe.sh, /readsecret.py), or stackstate component invokes webbrowser. Additionally, the runtime environment defeats reachability even if a transitive caller existed: the base image is registry.suse.com/bci/bci-micro:latest with no browser binaries, no DISPLAY, no $BROWSER, and the process runs as the non-root UID 1000 in group 1000 (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:60-66). The CVSS attack vector AV:L/UI:A also requires local access and a user clicking/triggering a browser open inside the container, which is not a supported workflow for this monitoring agent.

The image embeds CPython 3.13.13 via the omnibus build (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3, source tarball SHA256 f9cde7b0e2ec8165d7326e2a0f59ea2686ce9d0c617dbbb3d66a7e54d31b74b9). 3.13.13 was published 2026-04-07, before the 3.13 backport fix for CVE-2026-6019 was merged on 2026-04-21 (cpython commit 3c59b8b53fc75c7f9578d16fb8201ceb43e8f76c), so the vulnerable Lib/http/cookies.py:Morsel.js_output() is physically present on disk inside the image. The repository's only python3 patches are CVE-2025-6965-sqlite-3.50.4.patch and CVE-2025-8194-tarfile.patch (omnibus/config/patches/python3/); no local patch neutralizes Morsel.js_output. However, the sink is unreachable: a tree-wide ripgrep for 'js_output', 'Morsel', 'http.cookies', 'BaseCookie', and 'SimpleCookie' across Go sources, the rtloader Python bridge (pkg/collector/python/), the bundled python-scripts (omnibus/python-scripts/{packages,post,pre}.py), check definitions, and Dockerfile entrypoints returns zero hits in any execute path (only one match in pkg/serverless/trigger/testData/api-gateway-v2.json — a static AWS event fixture string, never deserialized via http.cookies). The agent exposes only DogStatsD UDP/8125 and trace-agent TCP/8126 (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:74) and a Go-based local status API; none of these surfaces emit HTML <script> blocks containing cookie values, which is the exclusive use case for Morsel.js_output(). Combined with the CVSS PR:H requirement (attacker must already control the cookie value being serialized) and the container running as non-root USER 1000:1000 (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile), there is no realistic call chain that can drive the vulnerable function with attacker-controlled input.

The image embeds CPython built from the upstream tarball pinned at omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3 (default_version "3.13.13"). CVE-2026-7210 affects every CPython release in the 3.10–3.15 series — fixed_versions is null in the intel because the fix commits 03794ce9, ccb8d2f7, and 24b8f12 (PRs python/cpython#149023, #149645, #149646) are still landing upstream. None of the patches under omnibus/config/patches/python3/ touch Modules/pyexpat.c or Modules/_elementtree.c (only CVE-2025-6965-sqlite-3.50.4.patch and CVE-2025-8194-tarfile.patch are present), so the vulnerable XML_SetHashSalt call site ships unmodified inside /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/lib/python3.13/lib-dynload/pyexpat*.so and _elementtree*.so. The bundled libexpat (omnibus/config/software/expat.rb:18, default_version "2.5.0") is also pre-2.8.0, so even the partial mitigation that depends on the wider salt API in libexpat 2.8.0+ is absent. However, the vulnerable sinks are unreachable from any shipped execute path: a tree-wide grep for `xml.etree`, `xml.parsers.expat`, `elementtree`, `pyexpat`, `xml.dom`, and `xml.sax` across cmd/, pkg/, omnibus/python-scripts/, rtloader/, and Dockerfiles/ returns zero hits — the only matches are dev-only invoke tasks at tasks/kernel_matrix_testing/ci.py:10, tasks/libs/common/junit_upload_core.py:7, tasks/libs/package/url.py:2, and tasks/kmt.py:15, none of which are packaged into the .deb extracted in Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:9. The agent's first-party shipped Python is limited to cmd/agent/dist/checks/ wrapper modules (network_checks.py, winwmi_check.py, prometheus_check/, libs/wmi/sampler.py) plus Dockerfiles/agent/secrets-helper/readsecret.py and omnibus/python-scripts/{pre,post,packages}.py — verified to import only json, argparse, os, and the stackstate_checks/datadog_checks base classes; no xml stdlib is touched. Default check configs under cmd/agent/dist/conf.d/ are Kubernetes-, container-, networking-, and Prometheus-oriented (kubernetes_apiserver.d, kubernetes_api_topology.d, containerd.d, container.d, network.d, etc.), all of which consume JSON or Prometheus exposition format rather than XML. Third-party integrations from StackVista/stackstate-agent-integrations (omnibus/config/software/stackstate-agent-integrations-py3.rb:36, version 7.71.2-3 per stackstate-deps.json:2) are similarly Kubernetes/container-focused and do not exercise the pyexpat/ElementTree code paths against adversary-controlled XML. The container also runs as USER 1000:1000 (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:67) with read-only-rootfs-friendly volume layout, but those are unrelated to reachability. Because no shipped Python code imports the vulnerable XML modules and no exposed port (8125/udp DogStatsD, 8126/tcp trace-agent at Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:71) accepts XML input, the hash-flooding sink cannot be driven by an adversary in this image.

The image embeds CPython built from the upstream tarball pinned at omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3 (default_version "3.13.13"), which is below the fixed 3.15.0 line, and none of the patches in omnibus/config/patches/python3/ address ftplib (only CVE-2025-6965-sqlite-3.50.4.patch and CVE-2025-8194-tarfile.patch are applied). The vulnerable Lib/ftplib.py:ftpcp ships unmodified in /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/lib/python3.13/ftplib.py. However, the sink is unreachable from any shipped execute path: a tree-wide grep for `ftplib`, `from ftplib`, `import ftplib`, `FTP_TLS`, `ftplib.`, and `ftpcp` across cmd/, pkg/, omnibus/python-scripts/, rtloader/, Dockerfiles/, and the embedded check wrappers returns zero hits. The single match on the literal string `ftp://` is at omnibus/python-scripts/packages.py:254, where it is a substring filter used to skip URL-style entries while parsing pip requirements files — no ftplib client is constructed and ftpcp is never invoked. The agent's first-party shipped Python under cmd/agent/dist/checks/ (network_checks.py, winwmi_check.py, prometheus_check/, libs/wmi/sampler.py), Dockerfiles/agent/secrets-helper/readsecret.py, and omnibus/python-scripts/{pre,post,packages}.py do not import ftplib, and the stackstate-agent-integrations bundle pinned at omnibus/config/software/stackstate-agent-integrations-py3.rb:36 (STACKSTATE_INTEGRATIONS_VERSION 7.71.2-3 from stackstate-deps.json:2) is Kubernetes/container/Prometheus-oriented with no FTP-based check. Independent of reachability, the ftpcp() SSRF requires the victim to actively call ftpcp() to relay a file between two attacker-influenced FTP servers (the bug is that ftpcp forwards the source server's PASV response into a PORT command on the target), which is a relay pattern the agent has no functional reason to perform. The image's exposed ports at Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:74 are 8125/udp (DogStatsD) and 8126/tcp (trace-agent APM) — neither speaks FTP and neither can drive ftplib code. The container also runs non-root as USER 1000:1000 (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:71), but that is incidental; the determinative fact is that no shipped code path reaches ftpcp.

statements.status_notes: Reviewed quay.io/stackstate/stackstate-k8s-agent:cb4d9ce2 on 2026-06-08. The embedded CPython 3.13.13 tarfile implementation is present, but the supported k8s-agent runtime does not extract attacker-controlled tar archives.
statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-7774 affects CPython's tarfile.data_filter path traversal protection when extracting crafted tar archives through tarfile.extract(), tarfile.extractall(), or callers such as shutil.unpack_archive(). The image embeds CPython 3.13.13 under /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/, so the vulnerable standard-library tarfile.py is physically present and Grype's CPE match is expected. Runtime inspection of quay.io/stackstate/stackstate-k8s-agent:cb4d9ce2 found no service startup script, cont-init script, agent entrypoint, or supported agent process that imports tarfile or calls tarfile.extract(), tarfile.extractall(), or shutil.unpack_archive() on external input. The supported agent runtime exposes DogStatsD on 8125/udp and the trace agent on 8126/tcp; neither endpoint accepts tar archives or drives Python archive extraction. The only meaningful extractall() path found in the image is in auxiliary OpenSCAP tooling, /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/lib/python3.13/site-packages/oscap_docker_python/oscap_docker_util.py, which is reached by the oscap-docker CLI and extracts a tar stream returned by the local Docker daemon for an operator-selected container/image. That tooling is not invoked by the supported Kubernetes agent runtime and requires explicit local/operator execution with Docker access, so it is outside the remote or unauthenticated adversary model for the deployed agent image. Generic packaging/build helpers that also reference extractall() are similarly not part of the running agent service path.
statements.action_statement: Defense in depth: remove OpenSCAP/build-time helper tooling from the runtime image if it is not required by supported agent workflows, and re-evaluate this VEX statement when the embedded Python runtime is upgraded or the agent runtime starts invoking tar archive extraction.

statements.impact_statement: The image bundles CPython 3.13.13 via omnibus (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:3 sets `default_version "3.13.13"`), which falls inside the affected range (>=3.11,<=3.14). However, CVE-2026-3298 is exclusively a Windows defect: the vulnerable sink `_overlapped_Overlapped_WSARecvFromInto_impl` lives in CPython's `Modules/overlapped.c`, a Win32-only extension that wraps the Winsock `WSARecvFrom` API and Windows `OVERLAPPED` structures. The companion Python wrapper `BaseProactorEventLoop.sock_recvfrom_into` in `Lib/asyncio/proactor_events.py` is only operative on Windows, where `ProactorEventLoop` is the default policy and the `_overlapped` C extension is built. The image under audit is a Linux container: the agent is produced from `Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:1-5` (Ubuntu 20.04 builder) with the runtime stage based on `registry.suse.com/bci/bci-micro:latest` (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:38), and the omnibus Python recipe takes the Linux branch (`unless windows_target?` at omnibus/config/software/python3.rb:25, configure+make build path through line 82) — the Windows-only `else` branch (lines 83-147, which calls `PCbuild\build.bat`) is not executed. Linux CPython builds do not compile `Modules/overlapped.c` and ship no `_overlapped` extension, so the vulnerable symbol is not present in the image's `embedded/lib/python3.13/lib-dynload/`. The only Python-side patches applied to this build are CVE-2025-6965 (sqlite) and CVE-2025-8194 (tarfile) per `omnibus/config/patches/python3/`; neither alters proactor/overlapped code paths. No Go source in the tree (e.g. `pkg/network/dns/driver_windows.go:33`, `pkg/network/driver/handle.go:159`) reaches CPython's overlapped module either, and those files are gated to Windows builds anyway. The image runs as USER 1000:1000 (Dockerfiles/agent/Dockerfile:74) on Linux kernels where `ProactorEventLoop` cannot be instantiated; an attacker has no path to invoke `sock_recvfrom_into` against the missing `_overlapped` extension.

statements.status_notes: Reviewed quay.io/stackstate/stackstate-k8s-agent:02f4adaa on 2026-06-10. The embedded CPython 3.13.13 is present, but no shipped code reuses a bz2.BZ2Decompressor instance after a decompression error, which CVE-2026-9669 requires.
statements.impact_statement: CVE-2026-9669 requires an application to catch the OSError raised by a failed bz2.BZ2Decompressor.decompress() call and then call decompress() again on the same instance. Source review of stackstate-agent 02f4adaaf3e60296d0e419a3b5213bca1710e886 found no Python bz2 usage: the only bz2 matches are Go archive/bzip2 code paths compiled into Go binaries (not the embedded CPython) and developer-only invoke tasks under tasks/ that are not shipped in the image. stackstate-agent-integrations has no bz2 usage. Image inspection of /opt/stackstate-agent/embedded/lib/python3.13 and /opt/stackstate-agent/checks.d found BZ2Decompressor referenced only by the CPython standard library itself: zipfile creates a fresh decompressor per archive member (_get_decompressor), bz2.BZ2File via _compression.DecompressReader constructs a new decompressor at init, rewind, and next-stream boundaries, and tarfile maps the OSError to ReadError without reusing the failed decompressor instance. No catch-and-retry pattern over the same decompressor object exists in the shipped image. Worst-case impact of the upstream flaw is an out-of-bounds stack write crashing the process (denial of service), not code execution.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade the omnibus-embedded CPython (omnibus/config/software/python3.rb) to the first 3.13.x release containing the fix from python/cpython#150600, then retire this statement.
